site.heroSubtitle: Full Stack Developer & Behavioral Product Researcher
site.heroQuote: I build products and study why people use them the way they do.
site.aboutBody:
I'm a full stack developer based in Vadodara, India, with hands-on experience building production applications for international clients across Canada, UK, USA, and the Netherlands. I work across the full stack — React.js, Next.js, Laravel, NestJS — from architecture to deployment.

On the side, I research and write about behavioral product psychology. I study how digital products manipulate human thinking and decision-making — mostly by observing myself making the same mistakes repeatedly. You can read my work on Substack.
site.footerTagline: © 2026 Shikhar Shukla · Built for curiosity. Powered by observation.

engineeringProjects.title: Booksly
engineeringProjects.description: United Kingdom Description: VAT reconciliation and data collection platform for a UK accounting client. Built solo as sole full stack developer — backend architecture, frontend integration, and AWS S3 file storage.

engineeringProjects.title: Appear Appraisals
engineeringProjects.description: Real estate appraisal platform with a 5-role access system and 900+ field property appraisal form, fully compliant with Canadian industry standards.

engineeringProjects.title: OPD.CARE
engineeringProjects.description: Healthcare platform with 4-role access system, online payment processing, QR code appointment booking, and multi-clinic support.

engineeringProjects.title: Shiv Trans Inc
engineeringProjects.description: Logistics and transportation website for a USA-based trucking company with live route display and operational data integration.

thinkingItems.title: "I Thought I Was Interested In Self-Knowledge."
thinkingItems.description: I started observing YouTube to understand myself. I ended up discovering what actually holds my attention.

thinkingItems.title: "The Day YouTube Stopped Choosing For Me."
thinkingItems.description: I thought I was testing YouTube's algorithm. The experiment ended up testing me.

thinkingItems.title: "How YouTube Controls Your Thinking Before You Notice."
thinkingItems.description: A breakdown of 7 design mechanisms YouTube uses to keep you scrolling — observed from the inside.

thinkingItems.title: "YouTube Is Not Your Problem. Tomorrow Is."
thinkingItems.description: First-person account of why I use YouTube to escape thinking about tomorrow — and what happens when I finally noticed.
